title: Restart the WCS From a System Cycle Stop Command Alarm
summary: Use the documented corrective action for the System Cycle Stop Command alarm to return from the Op Station Cycle Stop state. The source states that when WCS sends Cycle Stop to the Op Station, the corrective action is to press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI.
when_to_use: Use when the displayed alarm matches the documented System Cycle Stop Command alarm entry and the source-backed corrective action is needed to recover from the Op Station Cycle Stop state.
not_for: Do not use for alarms other than the documented System Cycle Stop Command entry.
Do not use when WCS HMI access is not available.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Confirm the alarm matches the documented entry, verify the documented cause/controller response, and press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI.

safety_notes: Use only the documented corrective action from the source.
The source does not provide a WCS HMI navigation path; do not improvise unsupported navigation or controls.
access_or_tools_needed: Alarm text confirmation from the HMI or alarm list

system_preconditions: The displayed alarm matches the documented System Cycle Stop Command alarm entry.
The Op Station is in Cycle Stop state as described by the source.

visual_references.description: Operator Station HMI VISU_ALARM screen showing tipper alarms and timestamps for confirming the alarm text.
Operator Station control panel screen showing cycle controls and alarm messaging context.
Read-only diagnostics screen for troubleshooting context if additional operator station status review is needed.

steps.title: Confirm the System Cycle Stop Command alarm is displayed
steps.purpose: Ensure the documented corrective action is being applied to the correct alarm condition.
steps.instruction: Review the alarm list and confirm the displayed alarm text matches "Left Tipper: System Cycle Stop Command" or the corresponding system cycle stop command entry.
steps.expected_result: The alarm text is confirmed as the documented System Cycle Stop Command entry.
steps.healthy_condition: The alarm list shows the matching System Cycle Stop Command alarm entry with timestamp information available.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed alarm does not match the documented System Cycle Stop Command entry or the alarm cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Review alarm list
actions.exact_action: Confirm the displayed alarm text matches "Left Tipper: System Cycle Stop Command" or the corresponding system cycle stop command entry.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_ALARM view showing tipper alarms and their timestamps; verify the alarm text matches the documented System Cycle Stop Command entry.

stop_or_escalate_if: The alarm text does not match the documented System Cycle Stop Command entry.
The alarm list cannot be accessed or the alarm text cannot be verified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Left Tipper: System Cycle Stop Command — Cause: WCS sends Cycle Stop to the Op Station. Controller Response: Op Station Cycle Stop state. Corrective Action: Press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI.
The Visu_Alarm screen displays a list of all tipper alarms along with a timestamp.

steps.title: Verify the documented cause and controller response
steps.purpose: Confirm that the observed condition matches the source-described cause and system state before applying the corrective action.
steps.instruction: Verify from the alarm table that the cause is WCS sending Cycle Stop to the Op Station and that the controller response is Op Station Cycle Stop state.
steps.expected_result: The documented cause and controller response match the observed condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The source-backed alarm entry confirms WCS sent Cycle Stop and the Op Station is in Cycle Stop state.
steps.failure_condition: The documented cause/controller response does not match the observed condition.

actions.label: Check alarm table entry
actions.exact_action: Verify from the table that the cause is WCS sending Cycle Stop to the Op Station and that the Op Station is in Cycle Stop state.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed condition does not match the documented cause or controller response.
The source table entry cannot be verified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Left Tipper: System Cycle Stop Command — Cause: WCS sends Cycle Stop to the Op Station. Controller Response: Op Station Cycle Stop state. Corrective Action: Press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI.

steps.title: Press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI
steps.purpose: Perform the documented corrective action for the System Cycle Stop Command alarm.
steps.instruction: Press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI as listed in the corrective action.
steps.expected_result: The corrective action is issued from the WCS HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: CYCLE START is successfully pressed on the WCS HMI.
steps.failure_condition: CYCLE START cannot be issued from the WCS HMI or access is unavailable.

actions.label: Issue cycle start
actions.exact_action: Press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cycle control area showing CYCLE START/CYCLE STOP controls for visual context of cycle control terminology.

stop_or_escalate_if: WCS HMI access is not available.
CYCLE START cannot be pressed or is not available on the WCS HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Corrective Action: Press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI.
The operator station control panel includes CYCLE START and CYCLE STOP controls for automatic operation.

steps.title: Observe whether the system leaves Cycle Stop state
steps.purpose: Verify that the documented corrective action resolved the alarm condition.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the system leaves the Cycle Stop state after the command is issued.
steps.expected_result: The system returns from the documented Cycle Stop condition after CYCLE START is pressed on the WCS HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The Op Station is no longer in Cycle Stop state.
steps.failure_condition: The system remains in Cycle Stop after the documented action is performed.

actions.label: Verify recovery
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the system leaves the Cycle Stop state after the command is issued.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Alarm list and timestamps for continued indication of the alarm condition or recovery observation context.
Operator station status/troubleshooting context if additional read-only observation is needed.

stop_or_escalate_if: The system remains in Cycle Stop after CYCLE START is pressed on the WCS HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Controller Response: Op Station Cycle Stop state. Corrective Action: Press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI.

success_criteria: The documented System Cycle Stop Command alarm condition is confirmed.
CYCLE START is pressed on the WCS HMI.
The system leaves the Op Station Cycle Stop state.
healthy_conditions: Alarm text matches the documented System Cycle Stop Command entry before action is taken.
The documented cause and controller response align with the observed condition.
After CYCLE START is issued, the system returns from Cycle Stop state.
failure_conditions: The displayed alarm does not match the documented System Cycle Stop Command entry.
The documented cause/controller response does not match the observed condition.
WCS HMI access is unavailable or CYCLE START cannot be issued.
The system remains in Cycle Stop after the documented action is performed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the system remains in Cycle Stop after the documented action is performed.
Escalate if the alarm cannot be matched to the documented System Cycle Stop Command entry.
Escalate if WCS HMI access is required but not available.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_ALARM screen showing tipper alarms and timestamps.
Cycle control area with CYCLE START and CYCLE STOP terminology.
Read-only operator station diagnostics for troubleshooting context.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Left Tipper: System Cycle Stop Command — Cause: WCS sends Cycle Stop to the Op Station. Controller Response: Op Station Cycle Stop state. Corrective Action: Press CYCLE START on the WCS HMI.

missing_details: The source does not provide the exact WCS HMI navigation path to reach CYCLE START.
The source does not state whether normal operators have WCS HMI access.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this recovery.
